Introduction to VaccineValidateR

Source: vignettes/intro.Rmd
intro.Rmd

VaccineValidateR

The VaccineValidateR package provides tools to clean, standardize, and validate childhood immunization data according to US ACIP recommendations and immunize.org guidance.

It supports:

  • Mapping local immunization codes to CVX codes
  • Selecting and filtering immunizations of interest
  • Evaluating dose validity, timing, and series completion
  • Generating population-level immunization metrics for research and quality improvement

Installation

You can install the development version from GitHub:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("psyllix/VaccineValidateR")

Then load the package:

library(VaccineValidateR)

Workflow Overview

The general workflow for vaccine validation is:

  1. Prepare a cohort of patients with create_cohort().
  2. Select immunizations using select_immunizations().
  3. Validate immunizations against ACIP rules using validation().
  4. Evaluate visits to identify missed or delayed vaccines with evaluate_visits().
  5. Generate metrics for reporting with population_metrics().

Example 

library(data.table)
library(VaccineValidateR)

# Example patient data
patients <- data.table(
  STUDY_ID = c(1, 2),
  DOB = as.Date(c("2015-01-01", "2018-05-10"))
)

# Example immunization data
immunizations <- data.table(
  STUDY_ID = c(1, 1, 2),
  PRODUCT = c("MMR", "DTaP", "Polio"),
  DATE_GIVEN = as.Date(c("2016-02-01", "2016-03-01", "2019-07-01")),
  CVX = c(3, 20, 10)
)

# Step 1: Create cohort
cohort <- create_cohort(patients)

# Step 2: Select immunizations
selected <- select_immunizations(
  immunization_data = immunizations,
  antigen_to_eval = c("MMR", "TETANUS", "POLIO")
)

# Step 3: Validate
validation_results <- validation(selected, patients = cohort)

# Step 4: Evaluate visits
visit_results <- evaluate_visits(visit_data = immunizations, antigens = validation_results$antigens)

# Step 5: Metrics
metrics <- population_metrics(patients = cohort, antigens = validation_results$antigens)

head(metrics)

Conclusion

VaccineValidateR enables transparent and reproducible workflows for immunization data validation, supporting both research and clinical quality improvement initiatives.

On this page

Developed by Jeremy J Michel.
